section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports edema in the lower extremities.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a symptom reported by the patient, not an observed exam finding, so it belongs in the Subjective section.

This is a known, objective diagnosis/problem rather than an interpretive assessment in this context; it could reasonably be listed among objective medical history, though its presence in Assessment is not highly misleading.

This reads as a factual condition (elevated glucose) rather than a synthesized diagnostic impression; it could fit better under Objective data, though its placement in Assessment is acceptable clinically.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient wants to continue meditation.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is the patient's stated preference/behavior, not a clinician order or management action, so it fits better in Subjective; its presence in Plan is not highly misleading.
